
(3.6)

Write an R function that takes U(0,1) random variables and generates random variables from the distribution with PDF f(x) = 1/4 if x in (-1,0) U (1,2), 1/2 if x in (0,1]

```{r}
library(dplyr)

rn_gen <- function(xs) {
ys <- case_when(xs <= 0.25 ~ 4*xs - 1,
                xs >= 0.75 ~ 4*xs - 2,
                xs < 0.75 & xs > 0.25  ~ 2*xs - 0.5,
                T ~ 0)
return(ys)
}

n <- 5000
xs <- runif(n, min = 0, max = 1)
ys <- rn_gen(xs)
hist(ys)

# KDE
#a <- density(ys)
#plot(a)
```

(3.7)

```{r}

plot_rm <- function(dist) {
  n <- 10000
  
  if (dist == "normal") {
      xs <- as_tibble(rnorm(n, 0, 1))
  }
  else if (dist == "cauchy") {
    xs <- as_tibble(rcauchy(n, 0, 1))
  }
  
  ys <- xs %>% mutate(running_mean = cummean(value),
                      row = row_number()) %>%
    select(row, running_mean)
  
  plot(ys, 
      type='l', 
      main='Sample means', 
      xlab='n', 
      ylab=expression(bar(Xn)),
      ylim=c(-1,1) )
  abline(0,0, lty=2) 
}

```
